question.text: Satish has a home loan of Rs. 30,00,000 at 6.5% p.a. (monthly rest) for 20 years. If the rate drops to 6.25%, his monthly EMI reduces from Rs. 22,367 to approximately:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At 6.25%: monthly rate = 6.25%/12 = 0.5208%. PMT(0.0625/12, 240, -3000000) = Rs. 21,928 approximately. **The Trap:** A 0.25% rate reduction might seem small, but over 20 years it saves approximately Rs. 1,05,360 (Rs. 439 × 240 months). **Cross-Exam Context:** EMI sensitivity to rate changes is tested in X-A (Ch 2, Ch 4) and is practical knowledge for all advisers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 0.25% rate cut saves ~Rs. 440/month on a Rs. 30 lakh loan — multiply by loan tenure to show total savings to clients.
